text:
REGIME — Risk-on, tech and financials led, staples and utilities lagged.

INDICES — VIX 19.1, falling; VIXY -3.80%. IWM +1.44%, QQQ +1.03%, SPY +0.98%, DIA +0.60% — small caps outperformed large. WTI oil (USO) was the standout cross-asset move at +2.92%.

SECTORS — XLK +2.10%, XLF +1.75%, XLY +0.91%, XLC +0.76%, XLI +0.71%, XLRE +0.47%, XLV +0.45%, XLB +0.44%, XLE +0.30%, XLP -1.00%, XLU -1.21%. Top industries: WGMI +5.85%, WCLD +5.44%; bottom: SLV -1.16%, XLU -1.21%.

THE 7s — Software +5.46%, Cloud +4.61%, Toll Road +2.76%, New Metals +2.65%, Medtech +2.11%, Insurance +2.01%, Semi +1.91%, Buildout +1.59%, New Staples +1.55%, Bank +1.18%, Mag +1.02%, Defense +1.00%, Fab +0.78%, Housing +0.72%, Industrial +0.42%, Energy +0.37%, Health +0.20%, Freight +0.09%, Power -0.59%, Old Staples -0.88%, Old Metals -0.91%. Strongest ticker in Software 7 not individually specified; weakest in Old Metals 7 not individually specified.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC Apr 29; CME shows 100% hold, 0% cut, 0% hike. Fed stance: 6 hawks (Cook, Hammack, Kashkari, Logan, Barkin, Goolsbee), 5 neutral, 4 doves. USD liquidity stress composite 54/100 (Elevated); most-stressed component is Reserve Scarcity at 80th percentile. TGA fell $99.3B; ON-RRP near zero at $0.2B.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 60.6, Greed. Top movers: WGMI +5.85% (Industries), Software 7 +5.46% (Baskets), WCLD +5.44% (Industries). 20d momentum: best sector XLK +6.57%, worst XLP -3.22%; best basket Semi +22.48%, worst Software -10.88%. Software 7 surged +5.46% on TSMC-driven tech optimism spilling into cloud and software names.

WATCH — LIT +2.72% (3.0x vol spike, unusual activity warrants follow-through check). XLP -1.00% (20d trend -3.22%, deteriorating vs risk-on tape). XLU -1.21% (worst sector today, -0.52% on 20d, rate-sensitive divergence with TLT +0.30% deserves scrutiny).
